tasks.title: RPC JSONL mode
tasks.definition_of_done: RPC mode accepts exactly one JSON command per stdin line, emits one JSON response or async event per stdout line, rejects malformed frames with structured errors, correlates responses with optional ids, and supports session, model, thinking, compaction, prompt/continue, and cancellation commands without live provider calls in tests; for prompt/continue-style commands, `success: true` means the command was accepted, queued, or handled, while failures after acceptance are emitted through the async event/message stream rather than as a second response for the same id; subprocess/NDJSON tests cover stdout framing, stderr diagnostics, exit behavior, interleaved async events, and compatibility with existing JSON mode semantics. Public RPC command/event schema, error semantics, cancellation semantics, and unstable 0.x protocol status are documented in crate docs or user-facing docs, with localized counterparts updated when applicable.

tasks.title: SDK embedding surface
tasks.definition_of_done: A Rust embedding API exposes shared SDK/RPC command and event types without duplicating protocol logic; tests drive the SDK with MockProvider through prompt, continue, session, model, thinking, compaction, and cancellation flows; public types are documented as unstable 0.x APIs, SDK docs do not make a stable `opi_agent::Transport` claim before 4.3 settles it, and no provider network access is required.

tasks.title: settle opi-agent::Transport

tasks.title: extension trait, lifecycle hooks, custom tools, custom commands, custom messages, and extension state
tasks.definition_of_done: An extension interface can register lifecycle hooks, custom tools, custom commands, custom agent messages, and scoped extension state while preserving agent_loop batching, hooks, tool validation, and session/event ordering semantics; tests cover registration, execution, blocking/error paths, state isolation, and serialization of extension-visible events without turning examples into core policy. Public extension API docs describe lifecycle ordering, hook error/blocking semantics, state serialization, custom tool/command/message contracts, and unstable 0.x status.

tasks.title: custom provider/model registration
tasks.definition_of_done: SDK or extensions can register custom providers and models so model resolution, --list-models, request construction, capability checks, and streaming all work through the existing provider/event contracts; tests use mock custom providers only, cover duplicate/invalid registrations, and prove no live provider calls are required. Public provider/model registration docs cover capability declaration, duplicate/invalid registration behavior, --list-models integration, streaming contract expectations, and the rule that provider breadth should arrive through registration rather than core provider additions.

session_notes.summary: Task 4.9 completed: SessionTree with BranchInfo (tip_id, depth, entry_count, timestamp, summary, fork_point) reconstructed from JSONL parent_id graph. BranchPickerState and BranchPicker widget for ratatui rendering with active-branch marker, selection highlight, metadata column. Active branch determined by last Leaf entry, falls back to trunk. tasks.title: streaming proxy
tasks.definition_of_done: A streaming proxy forwards command/event streams using the settled transport/RPC model, preserves framing and backpressure, propagates cancellation, redacts secrets, and is tested with mock streams for success, errors, malformed frames, client disconnects, and no live provider calls. Public transport/proxy docs cover framing, backpressure, cancellation, client disconnect behavior, secret redaction expectations, and unstable 0.x status.

4.evaluator_summary: Phase 4 audit blockers closed: RPC control semantics, tool-selection policy, runtime resource wiring, provider/model registry usage, Web UI RPC data consumption, package schemas, branch UI reachability, security/API hardening, docs synchronization, and full verification gates passing.

web UI implementation that consumes RPC/SDK events

audit_notes: Phase 4 exit recorded after audit remediation on 2026-06-05.
The exit covers the extensibility substrate and reusable web-facing component/state/rendering layer. It does not claim dynamic Rust plugin loading or a standalone browser application.
Symlink traversal tests are present but skipped on this Windows run when symlink creation lacks privileges (os error 1314).
